rulings.Case Title: CATHERINE HERRERA WATSON VS. TONY GARNICKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of And Motion Of Ccp 473(B) Attorney Affidavit Or Excusable Mistake, Surprise, Inadvertance To Vacate Order Of Dismissal Issued March 19, 2021 On Defendant Ma?S Motion
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jun 09, 2021 line 1. PLAINTIFF CATHERINE WATSON Plaintiffs Notice Of And Motion Of Ccp 473(B) Attorney Affidavit Or Excusable Mistake, Surprise, Inadvertance To Vacate Order Of Dismissal Issued March 19, 2021 On Defendant Ma'S Motion is continued to July 9, 2021 per June 4, 2021 order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANNA ESTRADA VS. SALLY LIU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Motion For Order Striking "Quasi Anti-Slapp" Labelled As Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint Filed By Cross-Defendant Anna Estrada And Timothy Workman And The First Amended Complaint Filed By Anna Estrada; Memo Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jun 09, 2021 line 2. CROSS COMPLAINANT SALLY LIU Motion And Motion For Order Striking "Quasi Anti-Slapp" Labelled As Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint Filed By Cross-Defendant Anna Estrada And Timothy Workman And The First Amended Complaint Filed By Anna Estrada is continued to July 30, 2021. On May 7, 2021 the Court instructed the moving party to provide a complete set of supporting papers for the motion separately bound. The Court received some, but not all papers. Therefore, the Court orders again that the moving party to submit courtesy copies of all filings in support of and in opposition to this motion. Courtesy copies to be delivered no later than May 21, 2021. Each set of papers in support of and in opposition to each motion shall be separately bound (rubber band or binder clip). The submission must be accompanied by a cover letter indicating the contents of each separately bound submission and the new hearing date. Parties may retrieve from Department 501 outbox and re-use in their new submission previously submitted courtesy copies. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
